summary: Implemented project directory layout with Deft.Project module
reasoning: Created Deft.Project module to handle project-scoped directory creation under ~/.deft/projects/<path-encoded-repo>/ with sessions/, cache/, and jobs/ subdirectories. Path encoding replaces / with - and strips leading -. Git worktree detection via 'git rev-parse --show-toplevel' ensures monorepos share a single project directory. Symlinks resolved via Path.expand/1 before encoding. All tests pass including comprehensive coverage of encoding, git root resolution, and directory creation.

summary: Implemented eval result storage with automatic cleanup
reasoning: Created Deft.Eval.ResultStore module for storing per-run results as JSONL with all required fields (run_id, commit, timestamp, model, category, pass_rate, iterations, cost_usd, failures). Implemented automatic cleanup to keep only last 30 runs. Created mix eval.export task for archiving results. Added test/eval/results/ to .gitignore.

summary: Major evals spec expansion based on 3-reviewer expert panel
reasoning: Deployed ML/AI eval expert, testing strategy expert, and product/UX eval expert in parallel. Key outcomes: increased iterations from 10 to 20-30, added holdout set requirement, added threshold tuning grid search methodology, moved format assertions from statistical to hard, added end-to-end task battery, added overnight loop safety evals, added bootstrapping strategy for pre-agent evals.

summary: Threshold tuning via grid search, not guessing
reasoning: Per-tool configurable thresholds with defaults determined by grid search over [2k,4k,8k,12k,16k,24k] tokens against 20 realistic tasks. Measure task completion vs context consumption, find the knee. Different tools have different information densities.

summary: Verification circuit breaker is highest-priority eval
reasoning: If the Foreman marks broken work as done, every other eval is suspect. This is the safety net for the entire pipeline. False positives here are the most expensive failure mode. Must be built before end-to-end evals.

summary: Rejected finding: turn counter off-by-one already fixed
reasoning: Agent reported turn_count not incremented on initial call, but specd_history.md shows this was already fixed — check changed from > to >= at line 1140, making total calls equal max_turns exactly.

summary: Rejected finding: async compaction is intentional design
reasoning: Agent reported async compaction doesn't protect triggering call. But async compaction was itself a fix (specd_history.md line 8) to avoid blocking gen_statem. The 70% threshold leaves 30% headroom — the triggering call won't exceed the window.

summary: Marked harness v0.1 as Implemented — no findings
reasoning: Audit found 0 confirmed findings. Both agent-reported issues were either already fixed (turn counter) or intentional design (async compaction). All spec requirements are met.

summary: ETS+DETS over human-readable files for cache/diary storage
reasoning: Cache and diary are runtime state, not user-facing data. Nobody needs to cat these files. Binary DETS is fine — tools interact through the GenServer API. ETS gives concurrent reads without process bottleneck. DETS handles crash recovery persistence.

summary: Replace Site Log with OTP messages + diary GenServer
reasoning: Site Log was fighting OTP — serializing to JSONL when message passing is the native coordination mechanism. The BEAM provides FIFO ordering, prevents collisions. Diary GenServer replaces data function, direct process messages replace coordination function.

summary: Diary is Foreman-write only, programmatic promotion via pattern matching
reasoning: Foreman has the birds-eye view. Programmatic rules (auto-promote contracts, decisions, corrections; never promote status/blockers) are cheap and predictable. No LLM call needed. Foreman can still write arbitrary entries via its own judgment for edge cases.

summary: Cache and diary are same GenServer module, different instances
reasoning: Same module (Deft.Store), different configs. Access control, write policy, and lifecycle are configuration, not code. One instance for cache (session-scoped, lazy writes, any agent writes), one for diary (job-scoped, sync writes, Foreman-only writes).

summary: Project-scoped directory layout under ~/.deft/projects/
reasoning: Mirrors Claude Code's layout. Each project (git repo) gets its own directory with sessions, cache, jobs. Path-encoded repo name as directory name. Issues stay in project .deft/ (committed to git), runtime state lives in ~/.deft/projects/ (not committed).

summary: Skills are explicit invocation only, no auto-triggering
reasoning: Context-based activation adds complexity and unpredictability. Explicit slash command invocation is simple and predictable. The agent can suggest a skill but cannot invoke one on its own.

summary: Commands are just markdown prompts, skills are structured YAML
reasoning: Two levels of complexity: commands are trivially easy to create (write a markdown file, drop it in directory). Skills are richer (YAML manifest + definition, progressive loading). Both invoked via slash command.

summary: Three-level cascade: built-in → global → project
reasoning: Mirrors config cascade. Users override built-in behavior without forking. Projects specialize without affecting global. Global skills in ~/.deft/skills/, project skills in .deft/skills/.

summary: Moved retry counter reset from :calling → :streaming to successful stream completion
reasoning: The retry counters (retry_count and retry_delay) were being reset at three transition points from :calling to :streaming (lines 285-286, 304-305, 323-324). This broke the retry logic: when a stream partially succeeded then errored, the retry would transition back to :calling, and on the next stream start would reset the counters to 0, allowing unlimited retries instead of the spec's 'up to 3 times' limit. Fixed by removing resets from those transitions and adding reset to handle_stream_done (when stream successfully completes). Retry counters are now only reset on: (1) successful stream completion, (2) new user prompt, (3) new provider call after tool execution, (4) max retries exceeded — all appropriate locations.

summary: Confirmed finding: retry counter reset on streaming transition allows infinite retries
reasoning: Agent.ex:284-286 resets retry_count to 0 and retry_delay to 1000 on every :calling → :streaming transition. If a stream partially succeeds then errors, handle_stream_error increments retry_count from 0, transitions back to :calling, and the next successful stream start resets it again. This bypasses the spec's 'up to 3 times' limit. Verified against actual code — the reset at line 285 is unconditional.

summary: Rejected 4 agent findings for harness v0.1
reasoning: (1) Turn limit off-by-one: already fixed per history, code uses >= correctly. (2) Spec missing Deft.Registry in process tree: spec mentions Registry in behaviors section, code correct, cosmetic gap. (3) Project context :system role after messages: Anthropic provider splits system messages via Enum.split_with at line 410, ordering irrelevant. (4) Async compaction: spec doesn't mandate synchronous compaction, async was intentional fix for blocking gen_statem (per history), 70% threshold leaves headroom.

summary: Fixed terminate_child calls to use PID instead of ref
reasoning: Task.Supervisor.terminate_child/2 requires a child PID, not a reference. The previous implementation at agent.ex:935 and agent.ex:950 was calling it with task.ref (a t:reference/0), which silently returns {:error, :not_found} without actually terminating tasks. Fixed by storing both task.ref and task.pid in task_info maps (for tool tasks) and data.compaction_task_pid (for compaction task), then passing the PID to terminate_child. This ensures abort properly kills in-flight tool and compaction tasks instead of orphaning them. Corrects the erroneous decision from 2026-03-17T03:02:33Z which incorrectly claimed ref was sufficient.

summary: Marked sessions v0.2 as Implemented — no new findings
reasoning: Audit found no new functional bugs. Two agent findings rejected: (1) ANTHROPIC_API_KEY not checked at startup — code defers check to API call time, which is correct behavior for non-API commands like deft config; (2) startup orphan cleanup absent — already tracked in orchestration v0.1 work list and blocked on Draft-status orchestration spec. Remaining work item (interactive mode) is blocked on tui v0.1 Draft, not a code bug.

summary: Added work item: terminate_child called with ref instead of PID
reasoning: cancel_state_operations (agent.ex:935) and cancel_compaction_task (agent.ex:950) pass task.ref to Task.Supervisor.terminate_child/2, which requires a PID. The call silently returns {:error, :not_found} and tasks are never terminated during abort. This is distinct from the previously-fixed KeyError crash (specd_history.md) — that crash was fixed by removing .pid access, but the replacement code uses the wrong argument type for terminate_child. Agent finding about turn_count stale in idle was rejected — works correctly since reset happens on next prompt arrival.

summary: Fixed Burrito release config to include wrap step
reasoning: Changed mix.exs:70 from steps: [:assemble] to steps: [:assemble, &Burrito.wrap/1]. This is required for Burrito to produce a self-contained single binary. Without the wrap step, mix release only runs standard Mix release assembly, producing a release directory instead of a portable binary. This implements the spec's distribution requirement (section 6: 'Packaged as a single binary using Burrito from day 1').

summary: Fixed compaction blocking by spawning Task for summarization
reasoning: Moved compaction summarization from synchronous receive loop to async Task spawned under ToolRunner supervisor. Added compaction_task_ref and pending_compaction_data to state. Handle completion via handle_event(:info, {ref, result}) and crash via handle_event(:info, {:DOWN, ref, ...}). Updated all state-specific {:DOWN, ...} handlers to check for compaction task. Extracted abort cancellation logic to reduce cyclomatic complexity. This allows abort, prompts, and DOWN signals to be processed immediately during compaction.

summary: Used Task.Supervisor.terminate_child/2 for abort handler
reasoning: Fixed abort handler crash by using Task.Supervisor.terminate_child/2 with task ref instead of Process.exit with pid. This approach is cleaner than storing pid in task_info, as Task.Supervisor properly manages task lifecycle and the ref is sufficient for termination.

summary: Promoted interactive session mode to work item, blocked on tui v0.1 Ready
reasoning: Audit finding recommended Option 2: defer until TUI is Ready. Since tui.md is Draft status, blocking is appropriate. Regular mode using TUI is implied by spec section 5.2 ("In non-interactive mode, no TUI is started"). Created work item with blocking dependency.

summary: Interactive mode stub sent to review — ambiguous TUI dependency
reasoning: deft with no args is a TODO stub (cli.ex:248-253). Spec section 5.1 requires it, but section 5.2 implies regular mode uses TUI (Draft status). Sent to specd_review.md for human decision on whether to implement minimal REPL or defer to TUI spec.

summary: Confirmed Burrito release missing wrap step
reasoning: mix.exs:70 has steps: [:assemble] but Burrito requires steps: [:assemble, &Burrito.wrap/1] per deps/burrito/README.md:135. Without wrap step, mix release produces a standard release directory, not a self-contained binary. The distribution feature is non-functional.

summary: Confirmed compaction blocks gen_statem process
reasoning: collect_stream_text_loop/4 at agent.ex:1266 does a synchronous receive loop inside the gen_statem for up to 30 seconds. During compaction, no messages are processed — abort casts, tool task completions, and DOWN signals are all delayed. This violates the spec's abort guarantee ('User can abort at any time').

summary: Confirmed abort handler crashes on task_info.pid KeyError
reasoning: task_info stored at agent.ex:930 as %{ref: task.ref} (ref only). Abort handler at agent.ex:246-248 iterates tool_tasks and accesses task_info.pid, which raises KeyError. Abort in :executing_tools crashes the agent instead of cleanly stopping tools.

summary: Rejected agent findings: turn counter (duplicate), context.build stub (OM Draft)
reasoning: Turn counter off-by-one already in specd_history.md. Context.build observation injection returning nil is expected — OM spec is Draft status, stub is the correct current behavior.

summary: Rejected agent findings: API key check, model_change, OM state, prompt extract, orphan cleanup
reasoning: API key: only relevant path (non-interactive) does check. model_change: no mechanism to trigger mid-session change exists, type is defined. OM state: OM is Draft. extract_last_user_prompt: keys: :atoms produces atom keys matching %{type: "text"} correctly. Orphan cleanup: depends on Draft orchestration spec.

summary: Implemented interactive session picking in deft resume
reasoning: Added numbered prompt using IO.gets/1 to select from listed sessions. On valid selection, delegates to execute_command({:resume_session, id}, flags). User can quit with 'q'. Invalid selections exit with error message. The session_list is stored to enable indexing after display.

summary: Wire bash_timeout config through to bash tool execution
reasoning: The bash_timeout config field was already defined and loaded but never propagated to the bash tool. Added it to agent_config, Tool.Context struct, and used it as the default timeout in bash.ex instead of the hardcoded @default_timeout_ms constant.

summary: Implemented streaming retry via :calling state transition
reasoning: To implement retry logic for streaming-phase errors, chose to transition from :streaming to :calling state rather than adding a separate retry handler in :streaming. This allows reusing the existing :retry_stream handler at agent.ex:375 which rebuilds context and re-calls call_provider_stream. Also updated stream process crash handler to use same retry path. Pattern matches handle_calling_error/2 behavior: reset partial state (current_message, tool_call_buffers), increment retry_count, exponential backoff, max 3 retries.

summary: Confirmed bash_timeout config never applied to bash tool
reasoning: Agent found bash_timeout is loaded into config and displayed by deft config, but never passed to agent_config or Tool.Context. Bash tool uses hardcoded @default_timeout_ms. Verified: cli.ex:429-436 omits bash_timeout from agent_config, tool.ex:60-61 has no bash_timeout field, bash.ex:52 uses args timeout or constant. User-configured bash_timeout is silently ignored.

summary: Confirmed deft resume missing interactive session picking
reasoning: Spec section 5.1 says deft resume should 'List recent sessions and pick one to resume.' Code at cli.ex:162-192 lists sessions then exits with instructions to use deft resume <id>. The 'pick one' interaction is missing entirely. Not blocked on TUI — a simple IO.gets prompt would suffice.

summary: Confirmed streaming-phase errors bypass retry logic
reasoning: Spec requires 'On provider error, retry with exponential backoff up to 3 times.' handle_calling_error/2 implements retry correctly for :calling state. But handle_stream_error/2 (agent.ex:720-738) transitions directly to :idle with no retry. Mid-stream errors from network blips or rate limits are silently dropped.

summary: Rejected: system messages hoisted to API system param
reasoning: Agent claimed format_messages collapsing all system-role messages into the system param loses ordering. Rejected: Anthropic API requires all system content in the system parameter — this is an API constraint, not a code bug. The logical assembly order from the spec is preserved within the system param.

summary: Rejected: turn count off-by-one (already fixed)
reasoning: Agent claimed initial LLM call not counted against turn_count. Already addressed in specd_history.md — fix changed > to >= in the limit check. Duplicate finding.

summary: Rejected: om_enabled missing from agent config
reasoning: Agent claimed om_enabled defaults to false causing incorrect compaction. Rejected: OM is not implemented (build_observation_injection returns nil). Treating OM as disabled is correct current behavior. Compaction should fire.

summary: Skipped: observation conflict resolution rules (duplicate)
reasoning: Already handled in prior audit — promoted to blocked work item under observational-memory v0.1 in specd_work_list.md. Decision in specd_decisions.jsonl dated 2026-03-16T21:02:00Z.

summary: Rejected: startup orphan cleanup missing
reasoning: Spec section 6 requires scanning for deft/job-* branches from crashed jobs. This is an orchestration feature — orchestration spec is Draft status. Can't clean up job artifacts when jobs don't exist yet. Not a sessions finding.

summary: Promoted %Done{} handler finding to work item
reasoning: Audit finding: %Done{} event silently dropped in :calling state due to missing handler clause. Falls through to catch-all. Recovery via :DOWN handler broadcasts misleading error message. Recommendation: add %Done{} handler in :calling that transitions to :idle cleanly (no error broadcast) for edge case where LLM stream completes without content. Low effort, eliminates misleading error message.

summary: Promoted observation conflict resolution rule to blocked work item
reasoning: Sessions spec section 3 item 5 requires conflict resolution rules in system prompt. Review finding: rules not implemented in current system prompt. Decision: defer to OM implementation (blocked item) rather than add rules about nonexistent observations, which would confuse the LLM. Added as blocked work item under observational-memory v0.1.

summary: Marked providers as Implemented — no functional findings
reasoning: Audited providers spec v0.2 against code. Agent reported format_messages return type mismatch and Done event handling. Rejected format_messages finding: the typespec mismatch is not functionally broken (format_messages is called internally by the provider's own stream/3). Done event handling is a harness concern (agent loop), not providers. No confirmed code bugs in provider layer.

summary: Sent observation conflict resolution rule to review — deferred until OM implementation
reasoning: Sessions spec section 3 item 5 requires observation conflict rules in system prompt. Code has generic rules without observation-specific language. Since OM is not implemented, adding rules about nonexistent observations would confuse the LLM. Filed as review item with recommendation to defer to OM implementation.

summary: Confirmed duplicate ToolResult messages on session resume
reasoning: save_tool_results writes Entry.ToolResult entries, then save_unsaved_messages also writes the tool_result user message as Entry.Message. On resume, reconstruct_messages converts both to Deft.Message structs, producing duplicate tool result user messages. This was introduced by the prior fix that added Entry.ToolResult reconstruction support. Filed as work item.

summary: Sent Done-in-calling-state edge case to review
reasoning: Agent reported %Done{} silently dropped in :calling state (empty LLM response). Recovery works via :DOWN handler but broadcasts misleading error message. Edge case is extremely rare and not clearly broken. Filed as review item with recommendation to add clean handler.

summary: Rejected 3 agent findings: turn counter (duplicate), project context order (matches spec), N separate tool results (subsumed)
reasoning: Turn counter off-by-one already in specd_history.md. Project context after conversation history matches spec section 4 exactly — system messages are extracted by format_messages regardless. N separate tool result messages on resume is part of the duplicate ToolResult finding.

summary: Rejected om_enabled finding — correct behavior while OM unimplemented
reasoning: Agent reported om_enabled missing from agent_config so compaction fires incorrectly. Rejected: OM is not implemented, so om_enabled=false (OM disabled) is correct. Compaction should fire when OM is disabled, which it is. When OM is implemented, the config will need updating as part of OM work.

summary: Convert Entry.ToolResult to user messages with ToolResult content blocks
reasoning: Updated reconstruct_messages/1 to filter both Entry.Message and Entry.ToolResult entries. Added entry_to_message/1 clause for Entry.ToolResult that creates user-role Deft.Message with a single ToolResult content block. Message ID uses 'tool_result_#{tool_call_id}' pattern to ensure uniqueness. This maintains Anthropic's turn structure contract: assistant messages with ToolUse blocks are now properly matched with user messages containing corresponding ToolResult blocks when sessions are resumed.

summary: Implemented extended thinking support in API requests
reasoning: Added thinking config parameter support as specified in providers spec v0.2 line 110. The stream/3 function now reads thinking: true and optional thinking_budget (defaults to 4096) from config, passes them to stream_loop, which adds the required Anthropic thinking parameter {type: enabled, budget_tokens: N} to request body. The existing ThinkingDelta parsing code (lines 220-222) is now reachable when API returns thinking content blocks.

summary: Added Usage event handler in :calling state
reasoning: The message_start SSE event emits Usage{input: N, output: 0} before first content arrives. The catch-all handler at agent.ex:347-349 was discarding it. Added explicit Usage handler in :calling state to call handle_usage/2, ensuring input tokens are properly tracked from the start.

summary: Fixed tool execution to use Task.Supervisor.async_nolink instead of spawn_monitor
reasoning: Prior spawn_monitor implementation provided crash isolation but never sent results back to agent — spawned process exited normally after computing results, agent received {:DOWN, ref, :process, pid, :normal} instead of {ref, results}, causing 'Tool execution was interrupted' error. Replaced spawn_monitor with Task.Supervisor.async_nolink under Deft.Agent.ToolRunner supervisor, which provides both crash isolation (async_nolink prevents crash propagation) AND result delivery (sends {ref, results} on completion). Also added fallback handling when ToolRunner supervisor is unavailable (returns error results immediately). Updated handle_tool_execution_complete to skip Process.demonitor when ref is nil.

summary: reconstruct_messages drops Entry.ToolResult — resumed sessions crash
reasoning: Store.reconstruct_messages/1 filters only Entry.Message, discarding Entry.ToolResult entries. Resumed sessions with tool calls have broken turn structure (ToolUse without matching ToolResult), causing Anthropic API errors. Confirmed at store.ex:199-207. Agent research claimed 4 findings; rejected 3 (test isolation issue, om_enabled default correct for now, String.to_atom is anti-pattern but not functional breakage). Confirmed 1.

summary: Extended thinking never enabled in API requests — dead code path
reasoning: Spec line 110 says thinking is supported when config includes thinking: true. Request body construction (anthropic.ex:86-95) never reads thinking config or adds the required Anthropic thinking parameter. ThinkingDelta parsing exists but is unreachable. Agent research found 5 potential findings; rejected 4 (duplicate ToolCallStart not triggered in streaming path, Map.get on struct is safe, double Done is harmlessly absorbed by catch-all, parse_event standalone usage not a current concern). Confirmed 1.

summary: spawn_monitor in tool execution doesn't send results back — all tool calls fail silently
reasoning: Prior fix (specd_history: replace Task.async with spawn_monitor) fixed crash propagation but broke result delivery. spawn_monitor spawns a process that computes results and exits; the agent expects {ref, results} (Task.async format) at line 451 but only receives {:DOWN, ref, :process, pid, :normal} at line 464, which is treated as a crash. Every tool execution fails with 'Tool execution was interrupted'. Confirmed at agent.ex:874-877 and 451-480.

summary: Usage events from message_start dropped in :calling state — input tokens lost
reasoning: Anthropic's message_start SSE event emits %Usage{input: N, output: 0} which arrives while agent is in :calling state (before any content). The catch-all at agent.ex:347-349 discards it. handle_usage is only called from :streaming state (line 414). total_input_tokens is never incremented from real data, session_cost underreports. Agent research initially flagged this as a providers finding but the provider correctly emits the event — the bug is in the harness dropping it. Rejected agent's streaming retry finding as ambiguous (spec transition table explicitly shows streaming→idle on error).

summary: Persisted Entry.Cost to session JSONL on usage updates
reasoning: The Entry.Cost struct existed but was never written to JSONL during handle_usage. On resume, cumulative cost was lost (reset to 0.0). Added calculate_cost/3 helper to compute cost from usage tokens using provider model pricing (input/output price per million tokens). Updated handle_usage to calculate turn cost, update session_cost in memory, and persist Cost.new(new_session_cost) via Store.append. Updated Store.resume to include session_cost field (via new find_latest_cost/1 helper). Updated Agent.start_link to accept optional :session_cost parameter. Updated CLI.start_agent to pass session_cost when resuming. Module-level alias added for Entry.Cost.

summary: Persisted Entry.Compaction to session JSONL after compaction
reasoning: The Entry.Compaction struct existed but was never written to JSONL. Added Store.append call with Compaction.new(summary_text, messages_removed) immediately after the compaction event broadcast in maybe_compact_messages (agent.ex line 1108). Also added module-level alias for Entry.Compaction to satisfy credo's AliasUsage check.

summary: Added Process.monitor/1 for stream PID with nil-safe guarding
reasoning: The agent stores stream_ref but never monitored it, causing hangs if the stream process crashes. Added monitor_ref = Process.monitor(stream_ref) in all places where stream_ref is set (:idle→:calling transition lines 191-203, retry handler lines 356-358, continue_after_tools lines 967-978, handle_idle_transition lines 776-787). Added :DOWN handlers for :calling (lines 370-385) and :streaming (lines 425-443) states that check monitor_ref match and transition to idle on crash. Added demonitor calls in handle_stream_done (line 639), handle_stream_error (line 663), and abort handler (lines 234-236). Used is_pid(stream_ref) guard because mock providers return references while real providers return PIDs - only monitor when stream_ref is a PID, otherwise set monitor_ref to nil.

summary: Fixed agent event pattern matching from tuples to structs
reasoning: The provider sends events as structs (%TextDelta{}, %ThinkingDelta{}, etc.) but the agent was pattern-matching on tuples ({:text_delta, payload}, etc.). Added Event struct aliases and updated all pattern matches in :calling state (lines 268-327) and :streaming state (lines 355-385) to match on struct patterns. The helper functions already expected map-like payloads with the correct keys, so they work with structs without changes.

summary: Marked tools spec as Implemented — no findings
reasoning: Audited all 7 tools against spec. Edit unified diff format difference (missing hunk headers) rejected as cosmetic — LLM consumer reads it correctly. Native fallback .gitignore gap already addressed in history (deliberate minimal approach). All spec-required features are implemented and working.

summary: Confirmed agent event handler format mismatch — critical bug
reasoning: Agent :calling and :streaming state handlers pattern-match on 2-tuples ({:text_delta, payload}) but the Anthropic provider sends struct events (%TextDelta{delta: text}). Structs are maps, not tuples, so all provider events fall through to catch-all handlers. The agent never transitions from :calling to :streaming. Rejected 3 other agent findings: turn_count off-by-one already fixed (>= check), spawn_monitor was acceptable per history, compaction collect_stream_text_loop correctly matches structs.

summary: Confirmed stream process monitoring missing — agent hangs on crash
reasoning: Providers spec section 1 requires agent to monitor stream process. History entry said to add Process.monitor but only spawn (vs spawn_link) was implemented. Agent stores stream PID but never monitors it. If stream process crashes, agent sits in :calling forever. Rejected parse_event/1 dead code finding as design choice (documented in comments).

summary: Confirmed 2 persistence gaps: compaction and cost entries never written to JSONL
reasoning: Entry.Compaction and Entry.Cost structs exist but are never used. maybe_compact_messages broadcasts event but doesn't call Store.append. handle_usage updates in-memory cost but doesn't persist. On resume, compaction history and cumulative cost are lost. Rejected 4 other findings: interactive resume depends on Draft TUI spec, tool_result rebuild is unnecessary (ToolResult blocks embedded in Messages), OM conflict resolution is OM-dependent (Draft), om_enabled default is correct for current state.

summary: Implemented piped stdin mode in CLI command dispatcher
reasoning: Added stdin detection using !IO.ANSI.enabled?() or :io.columns() == {:error, :enoent} as specified in the sessions spec. Positioned the stdin check in determine_command/2 after the -p flag check (for backward compatibility) but before other commands when no positional args are present. This allows 'echo prompt | deft' to work while preserving existing behavior for all other commands. Added Credo directive to allow higher cyclomatic complexity in determine_command/2 since it's a natural command dispatcher with many branches.

summary: Removed broken stateless parse_event code path for input_json_delta events
reasoning: Removed lines 342-349 from parse_event/1 that created synthetic tool call IDs (tool_#{idx}) for input_json_delta events. This code path was never used because content_block_delta events are handled by the stateful streaming layer (handle_content_block_delta) which has access to the real tool call ID from content_block_start. parse_event/1 is stateless and cannot provide correct IDs. Also removed dead code in handle_parsed_event for ToolCallDelta. Updated test to expect :skip and document why.

summary: Chose simplified ignored?/1 implementation over full .gitignore parsing
reasoning: Removed blanket String.starts_with?(part, ".") exclusion from ignored?/1 in both grep.ex and find.ex, keeping only explicit exclusions for .git, node_modules, _build, and deps. This simpler approach (option 2 from work item) allows searching in .github/, .config/, etc. without the complexity of implementing full .gitignore parsing.

summary: Modified truncate_output to track truncation and show temp file path when truncated
reasoning: Changed truncate_output/1 to return {truncated_output, was_truncated} tuple tracking whether truncation occurred via byte size OR line count. Updated format_result to accept was_truncated parameter and show temp file path whenever truncation happened, not just when file size exceeds 30KB. This fixes the bug where line-count truncation didn't show the temp file path.

summary: Implemented LLM-based compaction summarization with fallback
reasoning: Replaced static placeholder text with actual LLM call to summarize messages being compacted. Created helper functions: summarize_messages_with_llm/3 (builds prompt and calls provider), extract_text_content/1 (extracts text from message content blocks), and collect_stream_text_loop/4 (synchronously collects streaming response with 30s timeout). Falls back to static text if provider is nil or LLM call fails, ensuring robust behavior. Stream cancellation via Process.exit on timeout.

summary: Replaced Task.async with spawn_monitor for tool execution isolation
reasoning: Changed start_tool_execution/2 to use spawn_monitor instead of Task.async, providing crash isolation as required by spec. A crash in execute_tools_in_task no longer propagates to the agent process. Also updated abort handler to use Process.exit instead of Task.shutdown. Stored {pid, ref} as %{pid: pid, ref: ref} map to maintain compatibility with existing code that checks task.ref.

summary: Updated spec v0.2: removed project instructions from system prompt list — they belong in context assembly only
reasoning: Code puts project instructions in context assembly (separate message), not in system prompt. This is correct because project files can be large and benefit from separate message structure for better context management. Updated spec section 3 and section 4.1 notes to reflect this design.

summary: Updated spec v0.2: clarified SSE event mapping for content_block_start — these events are empty, first content arrives in content_block_delta
reasoning: Code correctly skips content_block_start events with text/thinking types because Anthropic's API doesn't include text content in these events. Emitting empty deltas adds noise. Updated spec section 4 SSE event mapping table to document this behavior: content_block_start (type: text/thinking) → (no event), first real content in content_block_delta.

summary: Marked standards as Implemented — no findings
reasoning: All spec-required behaviors verified: Makefile targets, mix.exs deps, .formatter.exs, .credo.exs, lefthook.yml, directory structure, test_helper.exs. Code matches spec with test.all correctly using --include flags.

summary: Task.async wrapping tool batch can crash agent — work item added
reasoning: agent.ex:719 uses Task.async (linked) to wrap the entire tool batch. If execute_tools_in_task crashes (e.g., get_tool_runner_supervisor raises), the linked exit propagates to the agent. Spec requires Task.Supervisor.async_nolink semantics. Inner ToolRunner correctly uses async_nolink but the outer wrapper defeats the isolation.

summary: Rejected turn counter off-by-one finding — fix is correct
reasoning: Traced execution: turn_count=0 on prompt, incremented in continue_after_tools, checked with >=. Initial call + 24 continuations = 25 total calls before pause at turn_count=25. Matches max_turns=25. The >= fix (recorded in history) is sufficient.

summary: Compaction uses static placeholder instead of summarization — work item added
reasoning: agent.ex:960-962 replaces compacted messages with a fixed boilerplate string containing no semantic content from the removed messages. Spec section 5 says 'summarize oldest messages and replace with a single summary message'. Context is permanently lost. This is a missing feature (LLM-based summarization).

summary: Bash temp file path not shown when output truncated by line count — work item added
reasoning: bash.ex format_result only shows temp file path when file size > 30KB. But if output is >100 lines and <30KB, it's truncated to 100 lines and the temp file (with full output) exists but its path is never reported to the LLM. The agent can't access the full output.

summary: Rejected edit tool empty new_string finding — Elixir empty string is truthy
reasoning: Agent claimed args['new_string'] with '' fails the && check in edit.ex:77. Verified: in Elixir, '' is truthy (only nil and false are falsy). '' && 'test' returns 'test'. The edit tool correctly handles empty new_string for deletion operations.

summary: Grep/find native fallback excludes all hidden dirs instead of .gitignore — work item added
reasoning: grep.ex:212-219 and find.ex:127-134 use String.starts_with?(part, '.') to exclude all hidden directories. This is not .gitignore behavior — it excludes .github/, .config/ etc. that may not be in .gitignore, and doesn't exclude non-hidden gitignored paths. The spec says 'Respects .gitignore' for the tool overall.

summary: parse_event/1 returns wrong tool_call_delta IDs — work item added
reasoning: anthropic.ex:342-349 parse_content_block_delta uses synthetic 'tool_N' IDs instead of real Anthropic IDs. The streaming path (handle_content_block_delta) correctly uses the tool_state index→ID mapping. But parse_event/1 is a public behaviour callback — callers get inconsistent IDs (ToolCallStart has real ID, ToolCallDelta has synthetic ID).

summary: Rejected content_block_start text/thinking delta findings — Anthropic doesn't send content there
reasoning: Spec maps content_block_start (text/thinking) to delta events, but Anthropic's API sends empty text in content_block_start and actual content only in content_block_delta. No data is lost. Added to specd_review.md for spec clarification.

summary: Piped stdin mode not implemented — work item added
reasoning: Spec section 5.2 requires 'echo prompt | deft' to read from stdin. cli.ex has no stdin detection or reading. History entry claims 'piped input via stdin' was implemented but the code has no IO.read or stdin handling. Falls through to :new_session which prints 'Interactive mode not yet implemented'.

summary: System prompt missing shell environment info — work item added
reasoning: Spec section 3.4 says system prompt includes 'current date, OS, shell'. build_environment_info in system_prompt.ex:122-134 includes date and OS but never reads or outputs $SHELL. The model lacks shell context for generating shell-specific commands.

summary: Rejected resume without -p and resume list interactive selection — blocked on TUI (Draft)
reasoning: Spec says 'deft resume' picks one to resume and 'deft resume <id>' resumes a session. Both require interactive mode which depends on TUI spec (Draft status, out of scope for sessions). Current implementation correctly shows summary/list and exits since TUI isn't available.

summary: Rejected atomize_keys String.to_atom finding — defensive concern not functional bug
reasoning: Config atomize_keys uses String.to_atom which could exhaust atom table with malicious config. But in normal usage config files have small fixed key sets. Spec doesn't prescribe atom safety. This is a code hardening concern, not a functional bug per audit bar.

summary: Project instructions placement ambiguous in spec — sent to review
reasoning: Spec section 3.5 says system prompt includes project instructions. Section 4.1.4 says context assembly includes project context as item 4. Code puts them in context assembly only (separate system message), not in the system prompt string. Spec appears contradictory — sending to review for human clarification.

summary: Implemented deft resume <id> with summary display and non-interactive continuation
reasoning: Added display_session_summary/2 to show last 10 messages in 'Role (HH:MM): first 100 chars' format. Extended execute_command to support -p flag for non-interactive continuation, reusing existing non_interactive_loop and agent startup logic. Updated start_agent/4 to accept initial_messages parameter for resuming sessions with existing conversation history. This unblocks the bootstrap checkpoint by allowing session resume without requiring TUI implementation.

summary: Changed spawn_link to spawn in stream/3 for graceful crash handling
reasoning: Replaced spawn_link with spawn to prevent stream process crashes from killing the agent. With spawn_link, when the stream process crashes, it takes down the linked agent process. With spawn, the agent can monitor the stream PID and handle :DOWN messages gracefully (transitioning to :idle or retrying), as specified in the providers spec section 1.

summary: Split message_start/message_delta usage tracking to match Anthropic API behavior
reasoning: Anthropic's message_delta event only contains output_tokens, not input_tokens. The input_tokens come from message_start event. Previous implementation incorrectly expected both in message_delta.

summary: Fixed tool call ID mismatch by maintaining index → real_id mapping in tool_state
reasoning: Refactored content_block_start, content_block_delta, and content_block_stop handling to maintain consistent tool call IDs. When content_block_start arrives with a tool_use type, we now extract both the index and real Anthropic ID (toolu_...), storing {:idx, index} → real_id mapping in tool_state. Content_block_delta events look up the real ID using the index before emitting ToolCallDelta. Content_block_stop uses the same mapping to emit ToolCallDone with the correct ID. This ensures all events for the same tool call use the same ID, allowing the agent to properly accumulate tool arguments. Extracted nested logic into helper functions to satisfy Credo's max nesting depth requirement.

summary: Fixed grep format_output/2 to count only actual match lines
reasoning: Changed format_output/2 to count only lines matching the filename:linenum:content pattern (using ~r/^.+:\d+:/) instead of counting all output lines. This prevents context lines, separators, and file headers from inflating the match count, ensuring the 'Results capped' message triggers correctly at 100 matches. Used the same regex pattern already present in truncate_to_match_limit/2 for consistency.

summary: Fixed grep global match cap with post-processing truncation
reasoning: Replaced ripgrep's --max-count flag (which limits matches per-file) with post-processing truncation that enforces a global 100-match limit across all files. Implemented truncate_to_match_limit that identifies match lines using regex pattern ^.+:\d+: and stops after collecting 100 match lines. This ensures consistent behavior where users always get at most 100 matches total, not 100 per file.

summary: Added token tracking and compaction fallback to agent state
reasoning: Implemented current_context_tokens and context_window tracking in agent state. Updated handle_usage to track context tokens from input usage. Added maybe_compact_messages helper that removes oldest conversation messages when context exceeds 70% of window and OM is disabled. Used simple heuristic-based compaction that estimates tokens per message and removes oldest messages until reaching 50% of window, replacing them with a summary message. Compaction is checked before each provider call.

summary: Fixed turn counter off-by-one with >= comparison
reasoning: Changed the turn limit check from > to >= to trigger at exactly max_turns calls. This approach is simpler than incrementing the counter in multiple places and avoids tracking complexity.

summary: Created Session.Worker supervisor with rest_for_one strategy and minimal OM.Supervisor stub
reasoning: The Worker supervisor needs to start Deft.OM.Supervisor as a child, but OM implementation is in future work items. Created a minimal stub that can be supervised and will be filled in later. Used Registry for process naming to allow lookup of agent and tool runner from within the session. Added name parameter to Agent.start_link to support registration.

summary: Removed Mix test alias, delegated exclusions to test_helper.exs and Makefile
reasoning: The test alias in mix.exs prepended --exclude flags to every mix test invocation, conflicting with --only flags in make test.eval/test.integration and breaking make test.all. The exclusions are better handled via ExUnit.configure(exclude:) in test_helper.exs for default behavior, with Makefile targets explicitly controlling inclusions/exclusions.

summary: Promoted CLI stub finding to work item: implement deft resume with summary + non-interactive continuation
reasoning: Recommendation Option 3 (resume summary + non-interactive continuation with -p flag) implements the spec requirements (section 1.3 display summary, section 5.2 non-interactive mode) without requiring TUI, unblocking the bootstrap phase.

summary: Sent interactive CLI stubs to review — ambiguous whether blocked on TUI
reasoning: deft and deft resume <id> are stubs that don't start agents. The sessions spec requires these commands but interactive mode needs user input, which may require TUI (Draft). Sent to specd_review.md with recommendation to implement resume summary + non-interactive continuation.

summary: Rejected finding: last_user_prompt key mismatch — Jason.decode(keys: :atoms) atomizes all keys recursively
reasoning: Agent claimed string/atom key mismatch in extract_prompt_text, but Jason.decode with keys: :atoms converts ALL nested keys to atoms, not just top-level. The pattern match %{type: "text", text: text} correctly matches the atom-keyed maps.

summary: Rejected finding: API key check missing on interactive paths — stubs don't make API calls
reasoning: verify_api_key/0 is only called in non-interactive mode. The interactive paths are stubs that don't start agents or make API calls, so the missing check has no functional impact today.

summary: Confirmed: stream/3 uses spawn_link instead of spawn_monitor — agent crashes on stream failure
reasoning: Spec requires agent to handle :DOWN messages for crash recovery. spawn_link creates bidirectional link; agent doesn't trap exits, so stream crash kills agent. Should use spawn_monitor or spawn + Process.monitor.

summary: Confirmed: message_delta usage parsing matches wrong fields — usage events never emitted
reasoning: Anthropic message_delta only carries output_tokens in usage, not input_tokens. Pattern match %{input_tokens => input, output_tokens => output} always fails. message_start (which has input_tokens) is not handled either. Token tracking is completely broken.

summary: Confirmed: tool call ID mismatch between ToolCallStart and ToolCallDelta/Done — tools get empty args
reasoning: ToolCallStart uses real Anthropic ID (toolu_...) but ToolCallDelta and handle_tool_call_stop use tool_#{idx}. Agent stores buffer under real ID but delta/done look up tool_N. Tool args never accumulated correctly; update_tool_call_args can't find matching ToolUse. Critical break.

summary: Rejected finding: content_block_start for text returns :skip — no functional impact
reasoning: Spec says content_block_start (type: text) should emit :text_delta. Code returns :skip. But Anthropic always sends empty text in content_block_start; actual content arrives in content_block_delta. Skipping the empty initial delta has no functional impact.

summary: Confirmed: grep format_output counts all output lines as matches — wrong count and cap detection
reasoning: format_output splits rg output on newlines and counts total lines. With context lines, separators, and file headers, the count is inflated. The 'Results capped' message triggers incorrectly.

summary: Confirmed: grep --max-count is per-file, not global — exceeds 100-match cap
reasoning: rg --max-count 100 limits matches per file. Searching across many files can return far more than 100 total matches, violating the spec's 'Caps at 100 matches' requirement.

summary: Confirmed: ToolRunner doesn't kill timed-out tasks — process leak
reasoning: After Task.yield_many returns nil for a timed-out task, the code returns an error result but never calls Task.shutdown. The task continues running indefinitely under the supervisor.

summary: Confirmed: bash Port.open {:spawn} splits on spaces, breaking sh -c for commands with spaces
reasoning: Port.open({:spawn, "sh -c 'command'"}) splits the entire string by spaces before exec. The -c flag gets 'command as its argument (split at space), not the full quoted command. Should use {:spawn_executable, "/bin/sh"} with {:args, ["-c", command]}.

summary: Confirmed: current_context_tokens not tracked, compaction not implemented
reasoning: Spec section 5 requires tracking current_context_tokens and context_window, with compaction at 70%. Agent state only has total_input/output_tokens. No compaction logic exists. Long sessions without OM will exceed context window.

summary: Confirmed: turn counter off-by-one — allows 26 LLM calls instead of 25
reasoning: turn_count starts at 0, initial call_provider_stream doesn't increment, continue_after_tools increments then checks > max_turns. First pause at turn_count=26, meaning 26 continuation calls + 1 initial = 27 total, or 26 if counting only continuations. Spec says 25.

summary: Confirmed: Session.Worker missing — ToolRunner supervisor never started, all tools return error
reasoning: get_tool_runner_supervisor always returns nil. execute_tools_in_task falls to else branch returning error for every tool call. ToolRunner module exists but is unreachable. Root cause: Deft.Session.Worker was never implemented.

summary: Rejected finding: Context.build observation injection hardcoded to nil — OM is Draft, not a functional break
reasoning: build_observation_injection/0 returns nil. OM spec is Draft status. Returning nil is correct behavior when OM is not implemented. The function signature will change when OM is built — that's expected, not a defect.

summary: Rejected finding: empty test/integration/ and test/support/ directories — expected during early development
reasoning: Directories exist per spec structure. No integration tests written yet because features are still being implemented. The test infrastructure is correct; tests will be added as features are built.

summary: Confirmed: Mix test alias breaks make test.all, make ci, and pre-push integration hook
reasoning: The mix.exs alias test: "test --exclude eval --exclude integration" prepends exclusion flags to every mix test invocation. make test.all (bare mix test) never runs full suite. Pre-push hook's mix test --only integration becomes mix test --exclude eval --exclude integration --only integration, which runs 0 tests. ExUnit.configure in test_helper.exs handles default exclusions correctly — the alias is redundant and harmful.

summary: Implemented Actor tool selection evals with 5 test cases covering read, find, grep, bash, and edit tool preferences
reasoning: Created test/eval/actor/tool_selection_test.exs per spec section 4.3. Each test sends a prompt to a real Agent with all 7 tools available, collects the first ToolCallStart event, and validates the tool name matches expectations. Tests run 10 iterations each with 85% pass threshold as specified. Used setup_all to verify ANTHROPIC_API_KEY and register provider. Agent is configured with minimal settings (turn_limit: 1, timeout: 60s) to execute quickly. Test cases: (1) 'Read src/auth.ex' should use read not bash, (2) 'Find all test files' should use find not bash, (3) 'Search for defmodule Auth' should use grep not bash, (4) 'Run the tests' should use bash, (5) 'Change foo to bar in config.exs' should use edit not bash. Tests are tagged :eval and excluded from default test runs per mix.exs aliases.

summary: Configured Burrito for single-binary builds targeting macOS and Linux
reasoning: Added releases/0 function with Burrito configuration for four targets: macOS arm64/x86_64 and Linux x86_64/aarch64. This enables self-contained binary distribution per spec section 6 requirements. The rg/fd startup check was already implemented in a previous iteration.

summary: Deferred piped stdin detection to avoid blocking
reasoning: Auto-detecting piped input in Elixir/Erlang is complex and can cause the CLI to block waiting for input. Users can use shell substitution like "deft -p \
" as a workaround until proper stdin detection is implemented.

summary: Implemented Deft.CLI with argument parsing and command dispatch
reasoning: Created CLI module using OptionParser for argument parsing. Supported commands: deft (new session), deft resume (list sessions), deft resume <id> (resume specific), deft config (show config). Flags: --model, --provider, --no-om, --working-dir, -p/--prompt (non-interactive), --output, --auto-approve, --help, --version. Fixed -p flag parsing by using alias p: :prompt instead of separate p: :string strict option (OptionParser doesn't handle standalone short flags with values well). Added external dependency check for rg and fd on startup with warnings if missing. Configured escript entry point in mix.exs with main_module: Deft.CLI. Interactive and non-interactive modes are stubs for now (will be implemented in later work items). All CLI commands parse and dispatch correctly.

summary: Implemented Deft.Config with YAML-based configuration cascade
reasoning: Created Config module that loads and merges configuration from four sources in priority order: CLI flags (highest), .deft/config.yaml in working_dir, ~/.deft/config.yaml, and defaults (lowest). Used yaml_elixir for YAML parsing with proper error handling for malformed files. Implemented deep_merge for nested config structures (e.g., om.enabled). Made user_home injectable via opts for testability while keeping production API clean. Validated all config fields in final struct with @enforce_keys. Added comprehensive test suite with 11 tests covering all merge scenarios, YAML edge cases, and nested config handling.

summary: Implemented session resume with message reconstruction and OM state restoration
reasoning: Added resume/1 function to Deft.Session.Store that loads JSONL entries and reconstructs conversation state. Messages are deserialized from entry content blocks back to Deft.Message structs with proper handling of both atom and string keys from JSON. Latest observation state and model changes are extracted for Agent initialization.

summary: Implemented session save with message and tool_result tracking
reasoning: Added saved_message_ids MapSet to track which messages have been persisted, and tool_execution_times map to capture tool timing. Messages are saved to JSONL before each transition to :idle state, and tool_result entries are saved separately with duration_ms calculated from start/end times. This ensures complete session persistence after each agent turn.

summary: Derived Jason.Encoder for all Entry structs and made deserialization handle both atom and string type values
reasoning: Jason encodes atoms as strings in JSON. To support robust round-trip serialization, all Entry structs derive Jason.Encoder, and deserialization accepts both atom and string values for the type field. This ensures compatibility across different contexts.

summary: Completed ToolRunner with tool dispatch map
reasoning: Updated execute_batch to accept a tools parameter (list of tool modules). Built a name->module dispatch map using Map.new with each module's name/0 callback as key. Updated execute_tool to look up tool by name in the map and call its execute/2 function. This allows ToolRunner to dynamically dispatch tool calls to the appropriate module without hardcoding tool names. Also updated Agent to pass tools from config to execute_batch, and fixed build_tool_context to return proper Deft.Tool.Context struct instead of map.

summary: Implemented Deft.Tools.Ls with formatted directory listing
reasoning: Created Ls tool using File.ls/1 and File.stat/1 to list directory contents with names, types, and sizes. Sorted entries with directories first, then files, alphabetically within each group for better UX. Formatted file sizes with human-readable units (B/KB/MB/GB) instead of raw bytes. Used single-letter type indicators (d=directory, f=file, l=symlink, ?=unknown) similar to Unix ls -l. Padded names to 40 characters for alignment. Handled errors gracefully: missing directories, non-directory paths, and stat failures (marking as unknown type). Returns empty directory message when no entries found.

summary: Implemented Deft.Tools.Find with fd and native fallback
reasoning: Created Find tool that uses fd when available for performance, falling back to Path.wildcard when not installed. Used System.cmd/3 for fd execution with appropriate flags: --type f to find files only (not directories), --max-results 1000 to cap results, --color never for clean output. Native fallback implements basic .gitignore support by rejecting common ignored directories (.git, node_modules, _build, deps, dotfiles). Both implementations cap results at 1000 files and respect .gitignore patterns. Sorted output files for consistent results.

summary: Implemented Deft.Tools.Grep with ripgrep and native fallback
reasoning: Created Grep tool that uses ripgrep (rg) when available for performance, falling back to :re + File.stream when not installed. Used System.cmd/3 for rg execution with appropriate flags: --max-count 100 to cap matches, --glob for file filtering, --ignore-case for case-insensitive search, --context for context lines. Native fallback implements basic .gitignore support by rejecting common ignored directories (.git, node_modules, _build, deps). Extracted nested formatting logic into helper functions (format_match, format_context_line) to reduce nesting depth from 4 to 3, satisfying Credo's strict mode requirement. Both implementations cap results at 100 matches and respect .gitignore patterns.

summary: Implemented Deft.Tools.Bash with Port-based execution and streaming
reasoning: Created Bash tool that spawns commands via Elixir Port for proper process management. Streams stdout/stderr in real-time to TUI via context.emit function. Configurable timeout (default 120s) with remaining time recalculated each receive loop to prevent timeout drift. Output truncation: first by bytes (30KB max), then by lines (100 max), handling trailing newlines correctly by separating line content from newline flag. Full output saved to temp file when truncated, with automatic cleanup of empty temp files. Shell escaping via single-quote wrapping with ''' replacement for embedded quotes. Created comprehensive test suite with 22 tests covering execution, streaming, timeouts, truncation, temp files, and shell escaping.

summary: Implemented Deft.Tools.Edit with two modes and file scope enforcement
reasoning: Created Edit tool with two modes: (1) string-match mode requiring unique old_string match, returning unified diff; (2) line-range mode replacing specific line ranges. Both modes enforce file_scope when set. Refactored validation and replacement into separate functions (validate_line_range, perform_line_range_replacement) to reduce cyclomatic complexity from 10 to below 9 per credo requirements. Implemented helpful error messages with nearby text suggestions when string match fails. Created comprehensive test suite with 26 tests covering both modes, edge cases, and file scope enforcement.

summary: Implemented file scope check using path normalization and prefix matching
reasoning: Used Path.expand/1 to normalize paths and String.starts_with?/2 to check if a path is within the allowed scope. This approach naturally allows subdirectories within scoped paths while rejecting paths outside the scope.

summary: Implemented Deft.Tools.Read with map-based media type lookup
reasoning: Created Read tool with file reading, pagination (offset/limit), line-numbered output, and image base64 encoding. Used module attribute map for media type lookup instead of case statement to satisfy Credo's cyclomatic complexity limit (max 9). Supports common image formats (.png, .jpg, .jpeg, .gif, .bmp, .webp, .svg, .ico, .tiff). Line numbers are 1-indexed to match editor conventions. Returns Text content blocks per spec.

summary: Implemented Deft.Provider.Registry GenServer with register and resolve functions
reasoning: Created GenServer to store provider configs in a map (provider_name -> module). Implemented register/2 to add providers and resolve/2 to look up provider module + model config by calling the provider's model_config/1 callback. Returns {:ok, {module, config}} or {:error, :unknown_provider/:unknown_model}. Added comprehensive test suite with 10 tests covering registration, resolution of valid/invalid providers/models, and integration with real Anthropic provider.

summary: Implemented format_tools/1 to convert Deft.Tool modules to Anthropic tools format
reasoning: Mapped Tool behaviour callbacks (name/0, description/0, parameters/0) to Anthropic's tools format with name, description, and input_schema fields. Simple Enum.map over tool modules calling the three callbacks. Added 3 comprehensive tests: empty list, single tool conversion, and multiple tools. All tests pass.

summary: Defined Deft.Tool behaviour and Deft.Tool.Context struct
reasoning: Created the Tool behaviour module with 4 required callbacks: name/0 (returns tool name string), description/0 (returns description for system prompt), parameters/0 (returns JSON Schema for args), and execute/2 (runs the tool with args and context). Defined Tool.Context struct with working_dir, session_id, emit function, and optional file_scope for orchestration. Used @enforce_keys to ensure required fields are always provided. Added comprehensive test suite with 5 tests covering behaviour implementation, execution flow, context creation, and emit functionality.

summary: Implemented format_messages/1 with smart system parameter handling
reasoning: Converts Deft.Message structs to Anthropic wire format per spec section 4. System messages with only Text content are joined into a single string for the system parameter (cleaner API format). Mixed content types use array format. All 5 content block types (Text, ToolUse, ToolResult, Thinking, Image) map to their Anthropic equivalents. Added 11 comprehensive tests covering all scenarios including edge cases.

summary: Validated parse_event/1 with comprehensive tests
reasoning: The parse_event/1 implementation existed but lacked proper test coverage. Added 12 test cases covering all event types from spec section 4: content_block_start (text/thinking/tool_use), content_block_delta (text_delta/thinking_delta/input_json_delta), message_delta (usage), message_stop, error events, unknown events, and malformed data. All tests pass, confirming correct implementation of Anthropic SSE event mapping to common Deft event types.

summary: Refactored SSE parsing to use parse_event/1 callback
reasoning: Moved event parsing logic from inline handlers into the parse_event/1 callback per spec. Piped SSE events from ServerSentEvents.parse/1 through parse_event/1 to normalize into Deft events. Extracted event handling into separate functions (process_single_event, handle_parsed_event) to reduce nesting depth from 4 to 3, meeting credo max_nesting requirement. Tool call state accumulation remains in streaming layer since it requires stateful tracking across multiple events (start, deltas, stop).

summary: Implemented Deft.Provider.Anthropic.stream/3 and cancel_stream/1 with SSE parsing
reasoning: Created streaming HTTP client using Req with into: :self to receive chunks as messages. Spawned separate process to handle HTTP connection and SSE parsing using ServerSentEvents.parse/1. Implemented event routing to parse Anthropic-specific SSE events (content_block_start, content_block_delta, etc.) and send normalized provider events to caller via {:provider_event, event} messages. Implemented cancel_stream/1 to terminate stream process. Refactored event routing into multiple function clauses to keep cyclomatic complexity below 9 per credo requirements.

summary: Implemented Deft.Agent.SystemPrompt.build/1 with comprehensive system prompt
reasoning: Created SystemPrompt module that builds system prompt from role definition, tool descriptions (via Tool behaviour callbacks), environment info (working dir, git branch, date, OS), and conflict resolution rules. Handles missing tools gracefully. Structured as sectioned markdown for clarity. Updated Context.build_system_prompt to use new module instead of placeholder.

summary: Implemented Deft.Agent.Context.build/2 with project context file loading
reasoning: Assembled message list in spec-defined order: system prompt (placeholder for now), observation injection point (nil until OM implemented), conversation history, and project context from DEFT.md/CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md. Implemented file priority (DEFT.md > CLAUDE.md > AGENTS.md) and reference resolution (CLAUDE.md can point to AGENTS.md). Created comprehensive test suite covering all scenarios.

summary: Validated prompt queueing implementation with comprehensive tests
reasoning: The prompt queueing functionality was already implemented in the Agent code (lines 67, 183-188, 595-638). Created test suite to validate: (1) prompts sent while agent is not :idle are queued using :queue.new/in, (2) queued prompts are delivered on return to :idle via handle_idle_transition/1. Tests confirmed queueing works correctly. Also discovered a limitation in queue delivery from state enter callbacks that will need addressing in future work.

summary: Implemented turn limit with pause-and-ask pattern
reasoning: Added turn counter that increments on :executing_tools → :calling transition, resets on new user prompts. When max_turns (default 25) is reached, agent broadcasts turn_limit_reached event and stays in :executing_tools state. User can respond via continue_turn(agent, true/false) to either reset counter and continue or return to idle. This prevents runaway loops while allowing user control.

summary: Refactored tool execution to use Task.async for abortability
reasoning: Changed from synchronous execute_batch call to Task.async wrapper, storing task struct in state. This enables abort handler to call Task.shutdown on in-flight tasks. Also extracted helper functions to reduce cyclomatic complexity per credo requirements.

summary: Used multiple function clauses instead of case statement for streaming events
reasoning: Refactored streaming event handler to use multiple handle_event clauses (one per event type) instead of a single clause with case statement. This reduced cyclomatic complexity from 10 to 1 per clause, passing credo's max complexity of 9.

summary: Created Provider behaviour with 8 event types
reasoning: Implemented Deft.Provider behaviour module defining the 6 required callbacks (stream/3, cancel_stream/1, parse_event/1, format_messages/1, format_tools/1, model_config/1) and 8 event type structs (TextDelta, ThinkingDelta, ToolCallStart, ToolCallDelta, ToolCallDone, Usage, Done, Error) per spec. Event structs use defstruct with @enforce_keys to ensure required fields are always present.

summary: Organized ContentBlock types as separate nested modules
reasoning: Created Deft.Message.Text, Deft.Message.ToolUse, Deft.Message.ToolResult, Deft.Message.Thinking, and Deft.Message.Image as distinct modules under Deft.Message. This provides clear namespacing, better documentation per type, and makes the union type explicit via @type content_block. All structs enforce required keys for safety.

summary: Installed Elixir 1.19.5 and lefthook 2.1.4 via Homebrew
reasoning: The verification task required running make setup and make check, which depend on Elixir/Mix and lefthook. Installed both via Homebrew on macOS. Also ran mix format to fix inline comment formatting in mix.exs per Elixir formatter conventions.

summary: Created .credo.exs with strict mode and required checks
reasoning: Implemented Credo configuration per spec section 5 with strict: true and five key enabled checks (WithCustomTaggedTuple, MixEnv, AliasUsage, CyclomaticComplexity, Nesting with max_nesting: 3)

summary: Interactive AI session for issue creation, structured JSON storage
reasoning: Humans are bad at writing structured data, AIs are bad at parsing unstructured data. The creation session lets the user speak naturally while the AI extracts structure (context, acceptance_criteria, constraints). The Foreman reads clean JSON — no markdown conventions or heuristic parsing. --quick flag for trivial issues.

summary: Single approval for deft work --loop
reasoning: User approves the first job's plan to verify Deft's approach. Subsequent jobs auto-approve. Balances oversight with autonomy — user doesn't need to babysit but gets a chance to course-correct early.

summary: 90-day compaction for closed issues
reasoning: Keep the JSONL file focused on active work. Git history preserves full record. 90 days is long enough to reference recent completions, short enough to prevent unbounded growth. Configurable via issues.compaction_days.

summary: Build native issue tracker instead of integrating Seeds/Beads
reasoning: Seeds validates JSONL+git approach but is Bun/TypeScript — shelling out adds runtime dependency. Deft already has JSONL+GenServer+ETS patterns from Site Log. Native Elixir implementation fits OTP architecture. Take concepts (ready queue, hash IDs, merge=union, atomic writes) but implement in-house.

summary: JSONL+git over Dolt or SQLite for issue storage
reasoning: Dolt is heavyweight (Go binary dependency) and solves distributed sync Deft doesn't need. SQLite adds a dependency for sequential access patterns. JSONL is consistent with Site Log and sessions, human-readable, git-diffable. merge=union gitattribute handles branch merges. Scale is small (tens-hundreds of issues).

summary: Minimal v0.1: no labels, no assignees, no templates
reasoning: Priority + dependencies cover the core need (what to work on next). Labels, assignees, and templates add complexity not justified until issue count grows or multi-agent assignment is needed. All are additive — easy to add later without breaking changes.

summary: Chunk-based decomposition, not file-based
reasoning: Real work has overlapping files. Foreman plans deliverables (1-3 Leads), not implementation steps. Dependency DAG + git worktrees handle integration. File ownership was too rigid.

summary: Runners are inline loops, different from Leads/Foreman
reasoning: Tasks can't own supervision subtrees. Runners making 3-10 LLM calls don't need gen_statem or OM. Asymmetry between agent tiers is acceptable.

summary: Lead actively steers Runners, is the memory bridge
reasoning: Lead is more trustworthy than OM for Runner context. Lead reads Runner output, evaluates, course-corrects. Pair-programming manager model.

summary: Worktree per Lead, conflict resolver merges
reasoning: Each Lead gets isolated worktree. Whoever has the conflict resolves it at merge time. Foreman merges in dependency order.

summary: Partial dependency unblocking via interface contracts
reasoning: Lead B doesn't wait for all of Lead A — just needs the specific contract (API shape). Foreman watches Site Log for contract entries and unblocks immediately.

summary: Remove suggested-response from Observer output
reasoning: Cheap model should not steer expensive Actor. Dynamic continuation hint provides continuity without the steering risk.

summary: Keep 30k first-activation threshold
reasoning: Lowering to 8-10k for short sessions not worth the extra Observer cost. OM is for long sessions.

summary: Observation sectioning promoted to requirement
reasoning: 10-agent review consensus. Named sections (Current State, User Preferences, Files & Architecture, Decisions, Session History) improve LLM attention, user readability, and Reflector compression. Current State at top gets highest attention weight.

summary: Keep Breeze for TUI despite risk
reasoning: User wants the LiveView-style API. Mitigation: build a streaming proof-of-concept early to validate performance. Fallback to Termite + custom rendering if it fails.

summary: No security/permission layer in v0.1
reasoning: User decision: focus on core functionality first. Tool permissions, sandboxing, secret redaction deferred to future spec.

summary: YAML for config format, not TOML
reasoning: TOML has poor Elixir ecosystem support (unmaintained libraries). YAML via yaml_elixir is actively maintained. JSON also acceptable.

summary: Burrito from day 1 for single binary
reasoning: Single binary distribution is a hard requirement. Accept the build complexity from the start rather than deferring.

summary: 40k reflection threshold validated by Mastra testing
reasoning: Mastra has empirical data showing 40k observation tokens work well with LLM attention. Context Expert recommended lowering to 20-25k, but real-world testing overrides theoretical concern.

summary: Replaced Observer/Reflector GenServers with Task-based architecture
reasoning: 10-agent review identified that long-running LLM calls in GenServers is an OTP anti-pattern (blocks the process, makes it unresponsive). Tasks spawned by State are simpler, cancellable, and don't require persistent processes.

summary: Per-session scope only for v0.1, cross-session deferred
reasoning: Cross-session memory (Mastra's resource scope) requires merging observations across threads and adds complexity around deduplication and conflict resolution. Per-session is the core value prop and sufficient for v0.1.

summary: Character-based token estimation over tokenizer dependency
reasoning: A real tokenizer (tiktoken) adds a NIF or large dependency. The 4:1 char/token ratio is well-known and sufficient for threshold decisions. Calibration from actual provider usage reports improves accuracy over time without adding deps.

summary: No embeddings or retrieval — always-present observations
reasoning: Retrieval-augmented memory requires the agent to know what it doesn't know. OM takes the opposite approach: everything observed is always injected. Token cost is managed by two-level compression (observation + reflection), not by selective retrieval. This matches Mastra's proven design.

summary: Named the project Deft
reasoning: Short, memorable, not an existing CLI command on Linux/macOS. Fits naming style of user's other projects (moto, specd). No major GitHub/npm/hex conflicts. Emacs has an elpa-deft package but it does not install a CLI binary.

summary: Chose Elixir/OTP over Pi SDK wrapper or TypeScript
reasoning: User wants stability, tailored flows, and natural concurrency for OM. Elixir's process model maps perfectly to Observer/Reflector as independent processes. Pi SDK wrapper would create an upstream dependency risk the user wants to avoid.

summary: Chose Breeze (Termite stack) for TUI over raw ANSI or other frameworks
reasoning: Breeze provides LiveView-style API that Elixir developers know, built-in components for scrolling/layout, and is actively maintained by a Phoenix core team member. Drafter and TermUI are alternatives but less proven. Raw ANSI is too much work for a proper chat interface.

summary: Chose gen_statem over GenServer for agent loop
reasoning: Agent loop has distinct states (idle, calling, streaming, executing_tools) with explicit transitions. gen_statem makes invalid transitions impossible at the framework level. Pattern from Opal validated this approach.

summary: Chose JSONL over SQLite for session persistence
reasoning: Sessions are read linearly (rebuild from start to end), append-only during operation, and human-readable for debugging. SQLite would add a dependency and is overkill for sequential access patterns.

summary: Scoped out MCP, sub-agents, and IDE integrations to future phases
reasoning: Core value is OM + stability. MCP and sub-agents add complexity without contributing to the differentiator. IDE integrations are a separate concern entirely.

summary: Learned from Opal but chose not to depend on it
reasoning: Opal validates Elixir/OTP works for coding agents (gen_statem loop, supervised tools, SSE streaming). But it's Copilot-only, hobby project, single author, very new — the exact instability risk user wants to avoid. Patterns are reusable without the dependency.

summary: Created harness as foundation spec, OM as separate spec
reasoning: Harness (agent loop, tools, TUI, sessions) and OM (observer, reflector, context injection) are distinct concerns with a clean process boundary between them. Separate specs allow independent iteration and implementation.
